title: roman_l2_job
text:
roman_l2_job is a self-contained pipeline that simulates Roman WFI Level-2 calibrated images over a chosen footprint, builds Level-3 skycell coadds and source catalogs from them, and measures how faithfully the recovered catalog reproduces the input catalog — a photometry recovery test. It was built to support simulated grism observations centered on a Wide-Field2 sub-region near (RA=10°, Dec=0°), but generalizes to any F158 footprint and any input source catalog.
The code, configs, and this report all live in the roman_l2_job GitHub repo.

title: roman_l2_job
text:
roman_l2_job is a self-contained pipeline that simulates Roman WFI Level-2 calibrated images over a chosen footprint, builds Level-3 skycell coadds and source catalogs from them, and measures how faithfully the recovered catalog reproduces the input catalog — a photometry recovery test. It was built to support simulated grism observations centered on a Wide-Field2 sub-region near (RA=10°, Dec=0°), but generalizes to any F158 footprint and any input source catalog.
The code, configs, and this report all live in the roman_l2_job GitHub repo.

title: roman_l2_job
text:
Contents

Pipeline — architecture, stages, data models, design choices (why certain scripts are vendored, how CRDS is hydrated, etc.).
Results — numbers from the full 324-sim run over the 6-visit / 2-segment footprint: depth distribution, completeness vs magnitude, flux residual, astrometric residual, PSF vs aperture photometry.
Discussion — what the numbers mean for the intended science use (grism support), with notes on depth geometry, Kron flux bias, and zodiacal background.

title: roman_l2_job
text:
Headline numbers






Note



From the full run (6 visits × 3 dithers × 18 SCAs = 324 F158 sims, 320 skycell coadds + catalogs, 4 h 5 min stage-04 on 4 m5a.2xlarge cores):

Median 50%-completeness (AB): 23.95 over all skycells, 24.05 in the deep-pixel subset, peaks at 24.50 in the deepest skycells.
Kron flux residual: +0.17 mag (recovered − input), unchanged across depth — it’s a Kron-photometry property on extended sources, not a pipeline calibration issue.
Astrometric residual: ~0.017″, sub-coadd-pixel across every skycell.
Depth distribution: only even integers appear in the per-pixel depth (2, 4, 6, 8), because pass 15 and pass 16 re-observe each pointing at the identical dither pattern — sum is always 2× an integer.
Field geometry: only ~25% of skycells within 0.5° of the field center hit the intended design depth; the full run is more a union of three visit-cluster cores than a uniform 1° disk.

title: roman_l2_job
text:
Pipeline overview






TipStage summary













Stage
Script
What it does




setup
00_setup.sh
Install STPSF data + CRDS scaffold


hydrate CRDS
00_hydrate_crds.sh
Pre-populate cache serially; avoids parallel-download races


verify CRDS
00_verify_crds.py
Size-based corruption check on ref files


catalog prep
00_prepare_catalog.py
Convert input catalog (mag or maggies) → catalogs/sources.parquet


filter pointings
filter_pointings.py
Carve a sub-region out of HLWAS.sim.ecsv


01 build
01_build_script.sh
Generate per-sim commands via a patched make_stack.py


02 sims
02_run_sims.sh
Parallel romanisim-make-image → L2 cal files


03 asn
03_build_asn.sh
skycell_asn on the L2s → one JSON per skycell


04 mosaic
04_run_mosaic.sh
Parallel MosaicPipeline → L3 _coadd.asdf


05 catalog
05_run_catalog.sh
Parallel SourceCatalogStep → _cat.parquet, _segm.asdf


06 compare
06_compare_catalog.py
Per-skycell recovery analysis; writes summary.csv + plots



Every stage is idempotent (skip-if-exists), takes configs/<tag>.yaml as its single argument, and logs under output/logs/.

title: Pipeline architecture
text:
Note



This page is a high-level narrative of the pipeline — what each stage does, why it’s shaped the way it is, and what files it reads and writes. For operator-level detail (sizing, parallelism math, CRDS troubleshooting), see CLAUDE.md in the repo. For the exact file-format schemas, see docs/data_model_reference.md.

title: Results
text:
Note



Numbers on this page are computed at render time from output/full/compare/summary.csv and the coadd .asdf files. Re-rendering the report picks up any re-run results.

title: Results
text:
Full-run configuration
324 sims over 6 visits (2 passes × 3 dithers × segments 490+521), 320 skycells after skycell_asn --product-type full.

title: Discussion
text:
Depth quantization. Why per-pixel depths only land on even integers (passes 15 and 16 re-observe identical pointings + dithers, so the sum is always 2×k).
Field coverage non-uniformity. Only ~25% of skycells within 0.5° of (10, 0) reach the design depth. The 0.5° cone is not the right way to think about the footprint — it’s a union of three visit-cluster patches plus the gaps.
Kron flux bias. The +0.17 mag under-measurement is a Kron-photometry property on extended sources; unbiased recovered flux for galaxies would use larger aperture (e.g. aper16_flux) or segment flux. Relevant for downstream science.
Zodiacal background. Measured 1.28 e⁻/pix/s, ~3.4× the polar-minimum reference. Expected for a field at ecliptic latitude −4° observed at elongation 128°. Already near the best achievable for this target — the field is structurally on the ecliptic, and the sim date is near the best observable elongation.
Grism implications. Discussion of what the recovered per-skycell depth and completeness mean for the grism observations the campaign is intended to support.
